title: Class with function named the same as the class raises an error
body:
A simple program like this works without issues:

```
program main;

    class test;
        function void print();
            $display("test");
        endfunction
    endclass

    test tst;

    initial begin
        tst = new();
        tst.print();
    end 

endprogram
```

But renaming the function to `test` (the same as the class name) throws an error:

```
program main;

    class test;
        function void test();
            $display("test");
        endfunction
    endclass

    test tst;

    initial begin
        tst = new();
        tst.test();
    end 

endprogram
```

Output:

```
iverilog -o test -g2012 test.sv
test.sv:4: syntax error
test.sv:4: error: invalid class item.
test.sv:5: syntax error
test.sv:5: error: invalid class item.
test.sv:6: syntax error
test.sv:9: error: invalid class item.
test.sv:11: syntax error
test.sv:12: error: invalid class item.
test.sv:13: syntax error
test.sv:13: error: tst doesn't name a type.
test.sv:14: syntax error
I give up.
```

Xcelium xrun 20.09 runs the second example without issues.
I don't know what the SystemVerilog standard says about this, but perhaps a warning similar to "You cannot name a class function the same as the class" would be good.

iverilog -o test -g2012 test.sv
test.sv:4: syntax error
test.sv:4: error: invalid class item.

test.sv:5: error: invalid class item.
